title: Daily Briefing — Jul 01
headline: Don’t let July’s VIX lull fool you—seasonal calm precedes turbulence.
body: Right, the big picture: Markets are grinding higher with SPY now at $746.77, sitting comfortably above all major MAs. Tech (XLK) keeps leading, and bulls will feel pretty smug—until you remember July is historically the VIX seasonal trough (R498). Calm tape, sure, but August-October is when vol expands and Q4 gets tense.

Rotation’s clear: XLK (Technology) leads with RS=122.9, but laggards are piling up—Financials, Healthcare, Utilities, and Staples all bark ‘high risk’ (risk levels HIGH, RS sub-100). XLK and QQQ are the only momentum plays worth watching, rest of the market looks tired. Watch out for XLI (Industrials) and XLY (Discretionary) as they’re improving but not yet leading.

Contrarian-wise, Fear & Greed is low at 30.5 (‘Fear’), nearing the opportunity zone (<25 is my trigger, not quite yet). But don’t get sucked in by today’s bounce in tech names like NVDA and AMD—the price is below daily 20 MA on most, and flows aren’t confirming aggressive accumulation. Seasonality (R529) says this is the midterm-year hiccup, so don’t treat calm as confirmation. Less trades = more wins here.
callout_body:
SPY $746.77 — watch for daily close above/ below 20 MA ($741.09)
NVDA $200.09 — still below SMA20 ($205.72), not a long yet
AAPL $289.36 — below SMA20 ($295.93), bears in control
AMD $580.91 — above all MAs, bull trend active
XLK $190.52 — triggered, momentum play, but seasonality caution
XLF $53.61 — near MA cluster, but lagging and risk high
VIX 16.45 — below SMA20 ($18.06), calm regime, VIX seasonal trough (R498)
Fear & Greed: 30.5 — opportunity building, but not extreme yet
action_items: Stay patient—don’t chase tech bounces under 20 MA (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL)
If XLK keeps momentum and closes above daily 20 MA ($186.55), consider rotational play, but size down—seasonal vol trap ahead
closing_quote: Patience, react, don’t predict—if it’s in the press, it’s in the price.
